title: idertia axillaris
intro: Idertia axillaris, commonly known as the "African mahogany," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial actions that contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Idertia axillaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as soothing digestive issues and alleviating respiratory problems. By utilizing its bioactive compounds, the plant offers potential remedies for various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
side_effects: Idertia axillaris can have side effects if used improperly, such as respiratory issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can cause dizziness, nausea, and headaches. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to liver damage and kidney strain. Improper use of Idertia axillaris can also exacerbate conditions like asthma and eczema.
